violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Kitchen prep cooler has absolutely no date marking completed on any stored foods. Breakfast items or lunch items. -Reach in refrigerator in back storage area has absolutely no date marking completed with pre-cooked eggs, waffles, and sausage patties stored in zip log bags.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -All single use food items in kitchen are stored vertical with potential for contamination from the cooking process. Advise storing upside down.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Kitchen floors and walls underneath/behind cooking equipment is greasy and needs cleaned.
Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. -Two bottles of degreaser are stored in contact with a cooking pan under hot hold equipment in kitchen.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Boxes of food stored on the floor of the walk in freezer.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Sanitation cloth was dry and no pail with sanitizer was present.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Observed a spatula and a tongs with food debris on surfaces and stored on unsensitized surfaces. Store utensils in a position that will not have contact with a unclean surface.

Reheating food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not reheated rapidly so all parts reached a temperature of 165F for 15 seconds within one hour. -Found hamburger patties in hot hold at 103 Degrees F. Other items in hot hold were at temp. When I inquired about the hamburgers it was stated that they came from the refrigerator and into the holding bin. They openly stated that they had not been reheated to a proper temperature and then place in hot hold. Their current process was not compliant with food safety guidelines by reheating food items to 165 degrees F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Multiple food bins were not date marked. Marking was inconsistent and varies in all locations.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -The utensils that were found had dried food debris on the food contact surface and were stored on nonsanitized surfaces.

Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items. Ceiling fixture in kitchen was missing the shield.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
